title: Glandulifolia serpyllacea
taxonomy: Glandulifolia serpyllacea belongs to the family Thymelaeaceae. This plant is a species of flowering plant. It is native to the Andes mountains in South America. The classification of Glandulifolia serpyllacea can be broken down further into the kingdom Plantae and the order Thymelales. Further research may be needed to confirm the exact classification.

Glandulifolia serpyllacea is not found but a plant known as Glandularia serpyllacea, also known as 'Sweet Marjoram', is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Verbenaceae). This plant is valued for its calming and soothing properties, with reported health benefits including reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Glandularia serpyllacea include essential oils, such as linalool and limonene, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Glandularia serpyllacea include teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which are often used to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
